name: Texas Exchange Bank
description_short: Community-focused FDIC-insured bank with locations in Crowley and Fort Worth, Texas, offering personal and business banking products since 1970.
description_long:
Texas Exchange Bank was founded in 1970 and opened its flagship location in Crowley, Texas in 1974. The bank expanded to Fort Worth in November 2010, maintaining its commitment to community-based banking and personalized service. As an FDIC-insured institution, deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing depositors with federal protection up to applicable limits.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking, savings, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and IRAs. For business customers, Texas Exchange Bank provides business checking, savings, money market accounts, CDs, and loan products. Both personal and business customers have access to online banking with robust digital tools, voice response banking, wire transfers, debit cards, ATM cards, and safe deposit boxes. The bank also provides online bill payment, check imaging, account transfers, and the ability to download financial data to popular accounting and financial software.

Texas Exchange Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local community commitment and personalized service. The bank proudly markets itself as "the only Crowley-based bank that has stayed committed to the community" and explicitly highlights its experienced bankers who provide what the bank describes as "personal and friendly service" with a "local touch." Bank employees are noted as active in social and civic activities. The institution positions itself as a growing community bank serving customers in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex with traditional relationship banking values.

The bank is best suited for customers seeking traditional community banking with in-person service at two physical locations in the Texas area. However, potential customers should note that the website provides no information about specific rates, fees, loan terms, or competitive product details. The bank's limited geographic footprint (two locations only) and lack of transparent pricing information online may be limiting factors for some consumers. As a smaller regional bank, Texas Exchange Bank may not offer the technological features or product depth of larger national banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Check imaging and online check ordering
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government protection
Long-established institution with 55+ years of operating history (founded 1970)
Offers both personal and business banking products including checking, savings, CDs, and IRAs
Comprehensive online banking with bill pay, check imaging, and account transfers
Two physical branch locations in Crowley and Fort Worth for in-person service
Loan products available for both personal and business customers
Ability to download account data to QuickBooks, Quicken, and Microsoft Money
Safe deposit box services for document and valuables storage
cons: No rate information published on website, requiring customers to contact bank directly for rate comparison
No fee schedule details provided online, limiting transparency about account costs
Only two physical locations limits accessibility for customers outside Crowley and Fort Worth areas
No mention of mobile banking app availability on the website
Limited information about specific loan products, terms, or eligibility requirements
best_for: Small business owners in the Crowley and Fort Worth, Texas area seeking personalized banking relationships
Community-oriented consumers who prefer local, relationship-based banking over large national banks
Customers who value FDIC insurance protection and established financial stability
diagnosis: Texas Exchange Bank is best for customers in the Crowley and Fort Worth area who prioritize personalized service and community commitment over digital innovation. The main caveat is the complete lack of transparent pricing and rates on their website, making it impossible to assess competitive positioning without direct contact with the bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
